title: NOC 62200 – Chefs | GTR Immigration
description: Chefs in Canada are more than just culinary experts; they are essential leaders in the country’s high-growth hospitality and tourism sectors. Under NOC 62200, chefs oversee professional kitchens in hotels, fine-dining restaurants, and corporate catering. As Canada continues to position itself as a global culinary destination, the demand for international chefs with specialized expertise is at an all-time high. This TEER 2 occupation offers a robust pathway to Permanent Residency (PR) through Express Entry and specialized PNP Hospitality streams.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level2Major Group62 – Retail sales and service supervisorsSub-Major Group622 – Chefs and cooksMinor Group6220 – Chefs and cooksNOC Code62200

duties: Plan and direct food preparation and culinary activities.
Supervise and coordinate the work of sous-chefs and cooks.
Create and modify menus to ensure quality and profitability.
Estimate food requirements and handle food/labor costs.
Ensure compliance with food safety and sanitation regulations.
employment_requirements: Red Seal certification is often required or highly preferred for senior roles.
Completion of a culinary arts program OR several years of experience in a commercial kitchen is mandatory.
Advanced food safety certification (e.g., FoodSafe Level 2).
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections suggest over 15,000 new job openings will arise as the tourism industry expands and the demand for high-end dining grows.
labour_market_demand: The Canadian culinary industry is currently facing a critical shortage of executive and sous-chefs. Provinces like British Columbia and Ontario have "Good" to "Very Good" outlooks, with a high volume of vacancies in major urban centers and resort destinations like Whistler and Banff.
Diverse Culinary Scene: Opportunity to work with international cuisines and farm-to-table concepts.
High Earning Potential: Senior chefs and executive chefs command premium salaries and benefits.
Path to Ownership: Excellent foundation for eventually opening your own restaurant in Canada.
pathways: Federal Skilled Worker (Express Entry): Direct eligibility for TEER 2.
BC PNP Skills Immigration: Specialized hospitality draws.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A key route for chefs in the Maritimes.
